title: inspect failing on some ghcr images
body:
Hi,

i'm using skopeo to check my images availability; for some reason, sometimes, `inspect` command dont work while the image is available :

`docker run quay.io/skopeo/stable inspect --retry-times 3 docker://ghcr.io/socialgouv/mon-psy-sante/app:1.69.4`

> time="2023-05-24T15:50:22Z" level=fatal msg="Error parsing manifest for image: fetching target platform image selected from image index: reading manifest sha256:f3840ef0a57f1467d7f126d1a64098333ad3eefeb7c18913298a5eb5454fe211 in ghcr.io/socialgouv/mon-psy-sante/app: manifest unknown"

Looks ok with `docker pull` though 

`docker pull ghcr.io/socialgouv/mon-psy-sante/app:1.69.4`

> 1.69.4: Pulling from socialgouv/mon-psy-sante/app
f56be85fc22e: Pull complete
8f665685b215: Pull complete
e5fca6c395a6: Pull complete
561cb69653d5: Pull complete
c5462cf75285: Pull complete
a4c0051e022b: Pull complete
265758bb4001: Pull complete
2805df4f0995: Pull complete
c528a6323221: Pull complete
5303f02a1256: Pull complete
a523bf56a68e: Pull complete
83fdfe89b3f9: Pull complete
3d49726c5211: Pull complete
480f655a2932: Pull complete
8da7a8e246da: Pull complete
Digest: sha256:7552f42192eabb7b3cf97d7cc1f0fa5750ede39a1ff531dc361b4c2ebee8ac78
Status: Downloaded newer image for ghcr.io/socialgouv/mon-psy-sante/app:1.69.4
ghcr.io/socialgouv/mon-psy-sante/app:1.69.4

What's wrong with this image ?

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

The registry says the the per-architecture manifest is not available; AFAICt that‘s not something that can be fixed client-side. The server-returned error message also does not provide any more details:

```
{"errors":[{"code":"MANIFEST_UNKNOWN","message":"manifest unknown"}]}
```

At least some very old Docker I have around fails similarly:
```console
# docker pull ghcr.io/socialgouv/mon-psy-sante/app:1.69.4
Trying to pull repository ghcr.io/socialgouv/mon-psy-sante/app ... 
manifest for ghcr.io/socialgouv/mon-psy-sante/app:1.69.4 not found
```
which version do you use ? i have `Docker version 20.10.21, build baeda1f`
`moby-engine-20.10.23-1.fc38.x86_64.rpm` fails exactly the same for me.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
Assuming this works correctly and the image is really missing on the registry, please reopen if there are indications that’s not the case.
